name: IBM Quantum Platform
description: IBM's cloud environment for accessing simulators, real quantum hardware, backend data, and experiment-management tools.
whyUseIt: It is one of the most direct ways to move from learning circuits to testing them against real-device constraints, calibration data, and queue realities.
engineerWorkflow: Inspect backend status, transpile circuits for a target system, run jobs, and compare the result to simulation and calibration context.
managerExpects: Disciplined experiment design and honest interpretation of noisy results.
demoDescription: Run a Bell-state or teleportation circuit on a simulator and then on a selected live backend to compare fidelity.
beginnerMistakes: Treating low-fidelity hardware output as proof that the algorithm concept failed
Ignoring queue, calibration, and connectivity constraints
